title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps · Language Clinic: Robots Failure Cache Policy
summary: The crawler fetches robots.txt from the wrong path, merges groups incorrectly, treats an outage as permission, obeys nonstandard delay blindly, and trusts cross-host sitemap URLs. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the robots failure cache policy increment in RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record. Learners progress through…
objectives: Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-robots-failure-cache-policy — Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-robots-sitemaps-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-robots-sitemaps-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_robots_sitemaps_3(robot_cases: int = 16, correct_decisions: int = 16, failure_modes: int = 5, sitemap_urls: int = 40, cross_host_rejections: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if robot_cases < 8 or correct_decisions < 0:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-cases"
    if correct_decisions != robot_cases:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-decision"
    if failure_modes < 4:
        return False, failure_modes, "robots-failure-policy"
    if sitemap_urls < 1 or cross_host_rejections < 1:
        return False, cross_host_rejections, "sitemap-boundary"
    assert correct_decisions == robot_cases
    return True, correct_decisions, "bounded-robots-sitemap-policy"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-robots-failure-cache-policy-dr5nqc
# Competency: scraper-robots-failure-cache-policy.
# Case 6588: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def evidence_scraper_robots_failure_cache_policy_dr5nqc(robot_cases: int = 16, correct_decisions: int = 16, failure_modes: int = 5, sitemap_urls: int = 40, cross_host_rejections: int = 3) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dr5nqc = "dr5nqc-cqn5rd-6588"
    assert evidence_variant_dr5nqc
    evidence_axes_dr5nqc = (
        "authorizationdr5nqc",
        "url6588",
        "responsecqn5rd",
        "treedr5nqc6588",
        "frontier6588dr5nqc",
        "reportcqn5rd6588",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dr5nqc)) == 6
    if robot_cases < 8 or correct_decisions < 0:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-cases"
    if correct_decisions != robot_cases:
        return False, correct_decisions, "robots-decision"
    if failure_modes < 4:
        return False, failure_modes, "robots-failure-policy"
    if sitemap_urls < 1 or cross_host_rejections < 1:
        return False, cross_host_rejections, "sitemap-boundary"
    assert correct_decisions == robot_cases
    return True, correct_decisions, "bounded-robots-sitemap-policy"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Robots Failure Cache Policy structural check is only one gate. RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
